perturbation.original_text: 3. Recipient Obligations . The Recipient shall protect and safeguard the confidentiality of all Confidential Information with at least the same degree of care as the Recipient would protect its own confidential information, but in no event with less than a commercially reasonable degree of care; not use the Confidential Information, or permit it to be accessed or used, for any purpose other than the Purpose, including without limitation, to reverse engineer, disassemble, decompile or design around confidenti al intellectual property; not disclose any such Confidential Information to any person or entity, except to the Recipient's Representatives who need to know the Confidential Information in relation to the Purpose and are informed of the obligations hereunder and agree to abide by the same. Recipient will promptly notify the Disclosing Party of any unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information or other breaches of this Agreement .
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Client Obligations . The User must protect and safeguard all Confidential Information with a reasonable degree of care; shall only use this Confidential Information for the Purpose, including without limitation, not to reverse engineer, disassemble, decompile or design around confidenti al intellectual property; not disclose any Confidential Information to any person or entity, except to the User's Representatives who need to know the Confidential Information in relation to the Purpose. User will promptly notify the Data Provider of any unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information or other breaches of this Agreement .
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Recipient' with 'User' and 'Disclosing Party' with 'Data Provider', while also altering 'at least the same degree of care as the Recipient would protect its own confidential information, but in no event with less than a commercially reasonable degree of care' to 'a reasonable degree of care,' creates ambiguity. The degree of care required is now less defined, and the original text's more specific standard contradicts the new, vaguer requirement. In-text contradictions are made on who are reffered on the Recipient's Representatives who need to know the Confidential Information in relation to the Purpose and are informed of the obligations hereunder and agree to abide by the same.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Return or Destruction of Confidential Information. Upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement, or at the Disclosing Party's request at any time during the term of this Agreement, the Recipient and its Representatives shall promptly return to the Disclosing Party all copies, whether in written, electronic or other form or media, of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information, or destroy all such copies and confirm the same in writing to the Disclosing Party ; provid ed, that the Recipient and its Representatives may retain such Confidential Information as is necessary to enable it to comply with its reasonable document retention policies.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Recipient' with 'User' and 'Disclosing Party' with 'Data Provider'. More importantly, it weakens the required standard of care for confidential information. The original required 'at least the same degree of care as the Recipient would protect its own confidential information, but in no event with less than a commercially reasonable degree of care,' while the changed text requires only 'a reasonable degree of care'. This introduces ambiguity and a potentially lower standard. The Contradicted Text mentions 'Recipient and its Representatives', directly contradicting the term 'User's Representatives', since 'Recipient' is replaced by 'User' and 'Disclosing Party' is replaced by 'Data Provider'. This alteration creates an in-text contradiction, as the standard expected of the 'Recipient and its Representatives' in the Contradicted Text is now unclear, and the responsibility of the User and its Representatives is now open to interpretation.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Required Disclosure. Any Disclosure by the Recipient or its Represent atives of any of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information pursuant to applicable federal, state or local law, regulation or a valid order issued by a court or governmental agency of competent jurisdiction (a " Legal Order ") shall be subject to the te rms of this Section. Prior to making any such disclosure, the Recipient shall make commercially reasonable efforts to provide the Disclosing Party with: (a) prompt written notice of such requirement so that the Disclosing Party may seek a protective order or other remedy; and (b) reasonable assistance in opposing such disclosure or seeking a protective order or other limitations on disclosure. If, after providing such notice and assistance as required herein, the Recipient remains subject to a Legal Order to disclose any Confidential Information, the Recipient (or its Representatives or other persons to whom such Legal Order is directed) shall disclose only that portion of the Confidential Information which, on the advice of the Recipient's legal counsel, such Legal Order specifically requires .
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Required Disclosure. Any Disclosure by the User or its Represent atives of any of the Data Provider's Confidential Information pursuant to applicable federal, state or local law, regulation or a valid order issued by a court or governmental agency of competent jurisdiction (a " Legal Order ") shall be subject to the te rms of this Section. Prior to making any such disclosure, the Customer must make commercially reasonable efforts to provide the Owner with: (a) prompt written notice of such requirement so that the Owner may seek a protective order or other remedy; and (b) reasonable assistance in opposing such disclosure or seeking a protective order or other limitations on disclosure. If, after providing such notice and assistance as required herein, the User remains subject to a Legal Order to disclose any Confidential Information, the User (or its Representatives or other persons to whom such Legal Order is directed) shall disclose only that portion of the Confidential Information which, on the advice of the User's legal counsel, such Legal Order specifically requires .
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Recipient' to 'User', 'Represent atives' to 'Representatives', 'Disclosing Party' to 'Data Provider', 'Customer' and 'Owner' creates ambiguity. This contradicts who is who, the document's requirements about providing notice and assistance are unclear due to the inconsistent terminology. The text creates doubt about the duties of the parties in the agreement
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Term and Termination. The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effect ive Date and shall expire 18 months from the Effective Date, provided that either Party may terminate this Agreement at any time by providing written notice to the other Party. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, each Party's rights and obligations under this Agreement , irrespective of termination of this Agreement , shall survive until the 18 month anniversary of this Agreement , even after the return or destruction of Confidential Information by the Recipient (the " Confidential Period "), provid ed that for any and all trade secrets of the Disclosing Party, the Confidential Period shall last for as long as such Confidential Information qualifies as a trade secret under applicable federal, state and/or local law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces ambiguity by altering the terms 'Recipient' to 'User', 'Represent atives' to 'Representatives', 'Disclosing Party' to 'Data Provider', 'Customer' and 'Owner'. This misalignment contradicts the contradicted text (Section 6) which refers to 'Recipient' and 'Disclosing Party'. Because Section 4 of the changed text no longer consistently defines the parties involved, it creates a contradiction with section 6 where it refers to who has to return or destroy Confidential Information. The changed terminology creates confusion and makes it unclear who is responsible for what obligations, constituting a strong in-text contradiction as defined by 'Misaligned Terminology'.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Return or Destruction of Confidential Information. Upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement, or at the Disclosing Party's request at any time during the term of this Agreement, the Recipient and its Representatives shall promptly return to the Disclosing Party all copies, whether in written, electronic or other form or media, of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information, or destroy all such copies and confirm the same in writing to the Disclosing Party ; provid ed, that the Recipient and its Representatives may retain such Confidential Information as is necessary to enable it to comply with its reasonable document retention policies.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Return or Destruction of Confidential Information. Upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement, or at the Data Source's request at any time during the term of this Agreement, the End User and its Representatives shall promptly return to the Data Source all copies, whether in written, electronic or other form or media, of the Data Source's Confidential Information, or destroy all such copies and confirm the same in writing to the Data Source ; provid ed, that the User and its Representatives may retain such Confidential Information as is necessary to enable it to comply with its reasonable document retention policies.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Disclosing Party' to 'Data Source' and 'Recipient' to 'End User', while keeping the original 'User' in the proviso, creates contradiction in text on who is responsible for returning and destroying confidential information. 'End User' and 'User' refer to different entities, leading to uncertainty about which party retains the right to keep confidential information for document retention purposes. This discrepancy creates confusion on the return and destruction of the Confidential Information
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. No Transfer of Rights, Title or Int erest . The Disclosing Party hereby retains its entire right, title and interest, including all intellectual property rights, in and to all Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The change introduces a contradiction because it renames 'Disclosing Party' to 'Data Source' and 'Recipient' to 'End User'. However, the proviso still uses the original term 'User'. This creates ambiguity about which entity ('End User' or 'User') has the right to retain confidential information, thereby directly contradicting the clause stating the 'Disclosing Party' retains all rights to Confidential Information, causing confusion and uncertainty, which fits the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 10. Non-Solicitation and Non-Circumvention. During the term of this Agreement and for a period of twelve (12) months after the expiration or termination of this Agreement, without the Disclosing Party’s prior written consent, the Recipi ent and its Representatives shall not contact or solicit an employee of the Disclosing Party for the purpose of hiring them, solicit the business of any client, customer or licensee of the Disclosing Party or outside of the ordinary course of business, dir ectly or indirectly contact or participate in communications with any disclosed companies, entities or persons (including each of thei r affiliates, parents or subsidiaries). Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, the Recipient and its Representatives shall not be restricted from hiring any employee of Disclosing Party who responds to a general solicitation for employment not directed towards the Disclosing Party’s employees.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. Non-Solicitation and Non-Circumvention. During the term of this Agreement and for a period of twelve (12) months after the expiration or termination of this Agreement, without the Information Provider’s prior written consent, the Receiver and its Representatives shall not contact or solicit an employee of the Information Provider for the purpose of hiring them, solicit the business of any client, customer or licensee of the Information Provider or outside of the ordinary course of business, dir ectly or indirectly contact or participate in communications with any disclosed companies, entities or persons (including each of thei r affiliates, parents or subsidiaries). Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, the User and its Representatives shall not be restricted from hiring any employee of Disclosing Party who responds to a general solicitation for employment not directed towards the Disclosing Party’s employees.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Disclosing Party' to 'Information Provider' and 'Recipient' to 'Receiver' while keeping 'User' and 'Disclosing Party' in the last sentence causes contradiction on whose employees the restriction applies to. It's unclear whether the restriction from hiring employees applies to those of the 'Information Provider' or 'Disclosing Party', creating confusion over the obligations. In-text contradictions are made due to this unclarity
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Client Obligations . The User must protect and safeguard all Confidential Information with a reasonable degree of care; shall only use this Confidential Information for the Purpose, including without limitation, not to reverse engineer, disassemble, decompile or design around confidenti al intellectual property; not disclose any Confidential Information to any person or entity, except to the User's Representatives who need to know the Confidential Information in relation to the Purpose. User will promptly notify the Data Provider of any unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information or other breaches of this Agreement .
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces ambiguity by altering 'Disclosing Party' to 'Information Provider' and 'Recipient' to 'Receiver' while retaining 'Disclosing Party' and adding 'User' in the last sentence of Section 10. This directly conflicts with the Contradicted Text, Section 3, which refers to 'User' and 'Data Provider' (previously Disclosing Party) having certain obligations. The change in terminology in Section 10 makes it unclear whose employees the non-solicitation clause applies to (Information Provider or Disclosing Party), which creates a strong contradiction with Section 3's reference to 'User' and 'Data Provider' as it relates to Confidential Information and breaches of the agreement. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type, which states that conflicting terminology creates uncertainty.

perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effect ive Date and shall expire 18 months from the Effective Date, provided that either Party may terminate this Agreement at any time by providing written notice to the other Party. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, each Party's rights and obligations under this Agreement , irrespective of termination of this Agreement , shall survive until the 18 month anniversary of this Agreement , even after the return or destruction of Confidential Information by the Recipient (the " Confidential Period "), provid ed that for any and all trade secrets of the Disclosing Party, the Confidential Period shall last for as long as such Confidential Information qualifies as a trade secret under applicable federal, state and/or local law.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Starting Date and shall expire 12 months from the Effective Date, provided that either Party may terminate this Agreement at any time by providing written notice to the other Party. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, each Party's rights and obligations under this Agreement , irrespective of termination of this Agreement , shall survive until the 12 month anniversary of this Agreement , even after the return or destruction of Confidential Information by the Recipient (the " Confidential Period "), provid ed that for any and all trade secrets of the Disclosing Party, the Confidential Period shall last for as long as such Confidential Information qualifies as a trade secret under applicable federal, state and/or local law.
perturbation.explanation: Here in this perturbation, the Effective date term of the agreement is being contradicted and shortened from 18 months to 12 months. This contradiction creates uncertainty on the term of the agreement. This creates problems and confusion. in-text contradictions about the true time of the agreement and if the parties are still obligated to the agreement
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Non-Solicitation and Non-Circumvention. During the term of this Agreement and for a period of twelve (12) months after the expiration or termination of this Agreement, without the Information Provider’s prior written consent, the Receiver and its Representatives shall not contact or solicit an employee of the Information Provider for the purpose of hiring them, solicit the business of any client, customer or licensee of the Information Provider or outside of the ordinary course of business, dir ectly or indirectly contact or participate in communications with any disclosed companies, entities or persons (including each of thei r affiliates, parents or subsidiaries). Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, the User and its Representatives shall not be restricted from hiring any employee of Disclosing Party who responds to a general solicitation for employment not directed towards the Disclosing Party’s employees.
perturbation.justification: The changed text shortens the agreement term from 18 months to 12 months. The contradicted text, Section 10, refers to obligations lasting for 'twelve (12) months after the expiration or termination of this Agreement'. The change to a 12-month agreement term directly contradicts the previous 18-month term, because Section 10 makes obligations last 12 months AFTER the agreement, meaning that parties now have different expected durations for their responsibilities. This constitutes a strong in-text contradiction as defined by the Misaligned Terminology definition.
